page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmc8y.32xlarge is a bare-metal instance powered by the custom Alibaba Yitian 710 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this server features 128 dedicated physical cores running at a clock speed of 2.75 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include bundled local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 64 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers. The instance does not feature dedicated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The arm64-based Yitian architecture offers high core density and power efficiency, which translates to qualitative cost advantages for highly parallelized workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated physical cores, this server is designed for compute-intensive workloads. Suitable applications include containerized microservices, high-concurrency web serving, distributed build systems, and parallel computing tasks that benefit from dedicated Arm cores without hyperthreading overhead.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmc8y.32xlarge is a bare-metal instance powered by the custom 128-core Alibaba Yitian 710 Arm-based processor. Operating on the arm64 architecture, this server provides 128 dedicated physical cores at 2.75 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a compute-optimized resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance utilizes the KVM hypervisor, features a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps, and contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. The custom Yitian architecture delivers high core density and power efficiency, offering a cost-effective profile for highly parallelized workloads. It is designed for compute-intensive tasks such as containerized microservices, distributed compilation, and high-concurrency web serving.
